text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver is a one-two punch - China factory activity beating on tech export demand, and the Iran/US deal pushing oil lower and fear down. That combination tends to lift semis and growth tech, which is basically your whole book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one that needs eyes on it today - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF, it decays, and your stop at $200 is about 15% below current price, which is a wide leash on a leveraged name when you're on margin. SMH (-0.69%) is mild red but watch it as the unleveraged cousin. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile as a fresh IPO with thin data - margin amplifies any sudden drop hard.

Watch: 1) SOXL holding above $200 stop if semis get any wobble. 2) Whether the China tech export demand headline actually moves SMH and SOXL today - follow-through matters.

Bottom line: Your book is set up fine for today's macro tone, but SOXL on margin with a wide stop is your biggest live risk - tighten or size it honestly.
plays: SOXL (-7.06%, stop $200): This is your most urgent conversation with yourself. You're down 7% on a 3x leveraged semi ETF, on margin - that's effectively amplified pain on amplified pain. The $200 stop is the floor. If China/Iran tailwinds hold today and semis catch a bid, that's your window to either tighten the stop (say, trailing up toward $215-220) or trim enough to sleep at night. Don't wait f

CRWD (+3.26%, scanner ACCUMULATE 8/12): You already own it and it's your strongest green name. Scanner sees a grade A setup with all three timeframes (daily/4H/1H) aligned up, entry zone $741-742, stop $740.31. That stop is extremely tight - it's basically 'it holds here or it doesn't.' If you want to add size, this is the cleanest pattern in your book today, but only if you're comfortable knowing

No new buys beyond the above - the scanner has setups on ALAB and AMAT but neither has a catalyst specific enough today to justify adding new names when you're already margin-heavy and have a losing leveraged position to manage first. Fix the house before buying furniture.
